title: AIIR Commit Receipt v1
description: Content-addressed cryptographic receipt for a git commit with AI authorship attestation. Schema: aiir/commit_receipt.v1

type.description: Receipt type identifier. MUST be the literal string 'aiir.commit_receipt'.

schema.description: Schema version identifier. MUST be the literal string 'aiir/commit_receipt.v1'.

version.description: SemVer version of the tool that generated this receipt.

commit.description: Git commit metadata and content hashes.

ai_attestation.description: AI authorship detection results for the commit.

receipt_id.description: Content-addressed receipt identifier: 'g1-' + SHA-256(canonical_json(CORE))[:32]. The 'g1' prefix denotes generation 1 of the ID scheme.

content_hash.description: Full SHA-256 hash of the canonical JSON of CORE_KEYS: 'sha256:' + SHA-256(canonical_json(CORE)).

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp of receipt generation. NOT part of CORE_KEYS — does not affect content_hash.

extensions.description: Extension point for downstream integrations. NOT part of CORE_KEYS — does not affect content_hash. May contain 'instance_id', 'namespace', or custom keys.

instance_id.description: Optional user-supplied instance identifier for multi-tenant deployments.

namespace.description: Optional user-supplied namespace for organizational grouping.

Commit.description: Git commit metadata. All string fields are sanitized: NUL bytes, terminal escapes, and URL credentials are stripped.

sha.description: Full hex SHA of the git commit (40 chars for SHA-1, 64 for SHA-256).

subject.description: First line of the commit message.

message_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the full commit message body: 'sha256:' + SHA-256(body).

diff_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the full git diff (streamed in 64KB chunks): 'sha256:' + SHA-256(diff).

files_changed.description: Number of files changed in the commit.

files.description: List of changed file paths (capped at 100). Present when --redact-files is NOT set.

files_redacted.description: Present (and true) when --redact-files is set. Mutually exclusive with 'files'.

files_capped.description: Present (and true) when the file list was truncated at 100 entries.

date.description: Git identity date (RFC 3339 or git default format).

is_ai_authored.description: True if any AI authorship signal was detected in the commit.

signals_detected.description: List of detected AI authorship signals (e.g., 'co-authored-by: copilot', 'signed-off-by: dependabot').

signal_count.description: Number of signals detected. MUST equal len(signals_detected).

is_bot_authored.description: True if the commit author/committer matches a known bot pattern.

bot_signals_detected.description: List of detected bot authorship signals.

bot_signal_count.description: Number of bot signals detected. MUST equal len(bot_signals_detected).

authorship_class.description: Classification of commit authorship. The Python validator additionally accepts legacy forms (ai-assisted, bot-generated, ai_generated) for backward compatibility with v1.0.4–1.0.13 receipts.

repository.description: Git remote origin URL (credentials stripped). Null when no remote is configured.

tool.description: URI-based tool identifier for SLSA/in-toto compatibility.
